Vector Database Comparison — 2023: Vector databases store high-dimensional embedding vectors and support approximate nearest-neighbor (ANN) search, enabling semantic similarity queries at scale. Leading options include Pinecone (managed cloud service), Weaviate (open-source, GraphQL interface), Qdrant (Rust-based, high performance), Chroma (lightweight, Python-native), and pgvector (PostgreSQL extension). Benchmark comparisons show Qdrant and Weaviate leading on queries-per-second for large collections, while Chroma and pgvector are preferred for smaller-scale or existing PostgreSQL deployments. The choice should be driven by scale requirements, operational complexity tolerance, hybrid search needs (combining vector and keyword search), and cost.
